_meta.purpose: Proposed keyword + negative-keyword additions to service_catalog to lift the auto-matcher's coverage on real historical quote lines. REVIEW ARTIFACT ONLY — apply manually after red-pen.
_meta.generatedBy: scripts/_propose-keywords.ts mining of 693 non-test quote lines since 2026-04-01
_meta.designRule: Prefer 2-3 word phrases. A 2-word phrase scores 3 (>=2.5) so it matches even when the parser's category-slug disagrees with the SKU's category (the dominant failure mode: e.g. a 'shelves' job tagged general_fixing while SHELF-FLOAT-01 lives in 'shelving'). Single common words were avoided unless category-anchored and distinctive.
_meta.applyNote: Each entry MERGES into the existing keywords[]/negative_keywords[] arrays (union, de-duped, lowercased). Does not replace.
_meta.schemaPerEntry: { skuCode, category, addKeywords[], addNegativeKeywords[], exampleDescriptions[], note }

exampleDescriptions: Install 65 inch TV on solid brick wall with customer supplied bracket
Install TV bracket onto solid wall
additions.note: Fixes a HUMAN-TAGGED anchor ('Mount TV to brick wall' -> TV-BRICK-01) that is currently unmatched. Existing kw 'mount tv brick' / 'tv brick wall' don't fire on 'Mount TV TO brick wall' (the 'to' breaks the contiguous phrase). Deliberately NOT adding bare 'brick wall' — it also appears in general_fixing lines ('Remove brick wall'), would be a false positive. TV-distinctive phrases only. Existing NEG (plasterboard/stud/drywall) retained.

exampleDescriptions: Hang two TV brackets to wall
additions.note: 'tv bracket(s)' with no wall-type stated -> default to plasterboard (the common UK case). TV-BRICK-01 NEG already excludes brick/solid/concrete, and the brick lines explicitly say 'solid'/'brick' so they route to TV-BRICK-01 first. 'tv bracket' is safe: probe shows it only appears in tv_mounting lines.

addKeywords: remove and dispose of old brick
exampleDescriptions: Site clean-up and full waste disposal
Remove and dispose of old brick fireplace.
additions.note: Biggest single win: 'Remove waste and rubbish' is a HUMAN-TAGGED anchor (WASTE-01) appearing 6x, all currently unmatched. 'waste and rubbish' (3 words, weight 5) is decisive and waste-distinctive. DELIBERATELY did NOT add bare 'dispose of' / 'remove and dispose' — those are too greedy (they stole 'Remove and dispose of laminate flooring' -> should be FLOOR-LIFT-01, and 'old plinths' -> PLINTH-01). Instead routed each disposal to its specific SKU (fridge->WASTE-APPLI-01, shed->SHED-REMOVE-01, plinths->PLINTH-01, laminate->FLOOR-LIFT-01) and gave WASTE-01 only the item-specific fireplace/polycarbonate phrases.

exampleDescriptions: Remove and dispose of fridge and freezer
additions.note: Appliance-specific so it beats the generic WASTE-01 'dispose of' on this line.

exampleDescriptions: Dismantle and remove old wooden shed.
Dismantle old shed and remove and dispose
additions.note: 'old shed removal' already exists; these cover the 'dismantle ... remove' phrasing. Distinct from SHED-INSTALL-01 (assembly) which keeps 'build/assemble shed'.

exampleDescriptions: Stain 6 fence panels to darker colour and gate, both sides
additions.note: 'paint garden fence' (3 words) is decisive and unambiguously painting-vs-fencing. Existing 'paint fence'/'fence paint' don't fire on 'Paint garden fence' because there's no contiguous 'paint fence' substring.

exampleDescriptions: Repaint marked wall adjacent to tiles
Re-paint full wall where scratched all over
additions.note: Single-wall jobs. 'paint wall'/'one wall' already keywords on PAINT-WALL-01 but the lines say 'full wall'/'medium sized wall'/'marked wall' (adjective between paint and wall, or 'repaint'). PAINT-ROOM-01 NEG already has 'one wall' so room won't steal these.

exampleDescriptions: Paint living room and kitchen walls
additions.note: MULTI-wall / whole-room. NOTE for reviewer: 'Paint two walls' is arguably PAINT-WALL-01 territory; I routed plural-walls and named-rooms to ROOM (the bigger job). 'paint office walls white' has plural walls -> room. Existing PAINT-ROOM-01 NEG (ceiling only / one wall / woodwork only) is preserved and is compatible.

'Paint a door' is a HUMAN-TAGGED anchor (PAINT-DOOR-01). Existing kw 'paint door' should already hit 'Paint a door' (text ' paint a door ' contains 'paint a door'? No — keyword is 'paint door', text has 'paint A door'). The 'a' breaks it. Add 'paint a door'. PAINT-DOOR-01 NEG (front door/external door) preserved so PAINT-FRDOOR-01 keeps front doors.

exampleDescriptions: Stain-block and repaint dark patch on wall
Stainblock and repaint 2 walls above window
additions.note: 'Stain-block and repaint wall' is a HUMAN-TAGGED anchor (STAINBLK-01). Existing kw 'stain block' (with space) misses the hyphenated 'stain-block' and the concatenated 'stainblock'. Adds both spellings. NEGATIVE 'mould' added so a combined 'Remove mould, stain-block and repaint ceiling' job stays on MOULD-PAINT-01 (a mould job is a mould job); verified the 4 pure stain-block lines contain no 'mould' so they're unaffected.

exampleDescriptions: Repaint external pagoda black and white

'refix kitchen plinths' covers the kitchen-tagged ones.

'window board' (singular) exists; line is plural 'window boards'.

rejectedAsRisky.reason: Appears in 3 general_fixing lines ('Remove brick wall','Extend brick wall','medium side mirror on solid brick wall'). Adding it to TV-BRICK-01 would wrongly match wall-demolition/mirror jobs. Used 'tv to brick'/'tv on solid' instead.
Too generic — 'install medium side mirror on solid brick wall' and others contain it. Used 'tv on solid wall'/'tv on solid brick' (tv-anchored) only.

Spans general_fixing, other, and waste sub-SKUs (fridge/furniture/shed). As a bare short phrase it would mis-route appliance/furniture disposals away from their specific SKUs. Kept 'waste and rubbish' (decisive) + 'remove and dispose' only as the catch-all, with appliance/shed phrases on the specific sub-SKUs so they win.
Single word 'panel' collides with bath panel (BTHPNL-01), fence panel, shower panel, plinth-adjacent. Used 'acoustic panelling'/'decorative panelling'/'wall panelling' multi-word forms instead.
Bare 'shed' spans install, removal, base, and painting ('Paint exterior shed'). Used 'install shed'/'new plastic shed' for install and kept removal/paint on their own SKUs.

Spans flat_pack (assemble), carpentry ('Cut headboard in half','Install 3 upholstered headboards'), furniture_repair (re-upholster). Too ambiguous as a bare keyword. Left most headboard lines bespoke; only 'bed and headboard' (assembly context) added to FP-BED-01. Upholstered-headboard build/recover lines flagged as a possible new SKU.
Bare 'hinge' would grab window-hinge lines (no SKU) and wardrobe-hinge. Used door/cupboard-anchored phrases; window hinges flagged as a catalog gap.

Bare 'socket' spans swap vs add-new (SCKT-NEW-01) vs carpentry ('Cut out socket opening in melamine board'). SCKT-SWAP-01 NEG already lists add/new/extra; kept swap-specific multi-word phrases and did NOT add bare 'socket'.

Genuinely ambiguous between filler (FILL-HOLE-01) and replastering (PLAST-01). Split by qualifier: 'epoxy filler'/'expanding foam' -> FILL-HOLE-01; 'replaster'/'patch hole in wall'/'kitchen ceiling hole' -> PLAST-01. Did not add bare 'patch hole' to both (would double-count / could mis-route by category).
Spans wall panelling, shower wall panelling, bath panelling. Used context-qualified phrases ('acoustic panelling','bath panelling','shower panelling') per SKU rather than bare 'panelling'.
